column2: Promote four PR #86 canon docs from status: proposed to status: active
column3: Four canon docs landed in PR #86 (merged 2026-05-13) at status: proposed, stability: draft — ams-output-artifact-validator (persona), output-artifact-format-validation-via-spawned-agent-session (constraint), ams-oddkit-gauntlet-runner (persona), oddkit-gauntlet-via-spawned-agent-session (constraint). This PR flips all four to status: active. AMS convention research: ams://docs/proposals-governance governs the P00xx → D00xx track only — it covers proposals under canon/proposals/ that get renumbered and moved on promotion. The four PR #86 docs are tier-2 constraints and personas, not P-numbered proposals, and live directly under canon/constraints/ and canon/personas/. Field-evidence from sibling canon-code-sync-via-spawned-agent-session.md (the constraint these four extend) shows the AMS convention for non-P-numbered canon: settled docs carry status: active. Stability stays at draft until probe parity data accumulates; the active flag promotes the binding contract while reserving signal for the stability bump.

column2: This PR is also the first runtime-probe firing event against the fully-wired substrate
column3: Three runtime probes were wired in PR #86 but no PR has been opened in this repository since the merge, so the probes have never fired. The operator's lane for this session locks both objectives into a single PR: promote the four PR #86 canon docs AND produce the first PR that triggers all three probes against the now-fully-wired substrate (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY worker secret deployed; 3-persona allow-list deployed). The legacy canon-code-sync-audit.yml continues to gate. The three runtime probes are expected to post non-gating verdict comments. This is the first real parity datapoint between the legacy Managed Agents path and the runtime-substrate path.

column2: Stability stays at draft until probe parity data accumulates
column3: The four promoted docs keep stability: draft. Field-evidence from canon-code-sync-via-spawned-agent-session.md sets the precedent: a constraint with status: active can carry stability: semi_stable once it has earned operational evidence (real probe runs, real parity comparisons, real cutover decisions). The four docs promoted here have zero probe runs against them as of this PR's open moment. Bumping stability before evidence exists would falsify the signal that stability is meant to carry. A follow-up canon edit lifts stability from draft to semi_stable once enough probe-firing observations accumulate to justify the bump.

column2: AMS canon status taxonomy for non-P-numbered docs is direct proposed → active
column3: Documented for next-session clarity. canon/proposals/PNNNN-*.md files use the lifecycle from ams://docs/proposals-governance: proposed → accepted (transient during promotion PR) → promoted (file removed; new DNNNN file lands in canon/decisions/) or → rejected / withdrawn. canon/constraints/*.md and canon/personas/*.md files use a simpler direct lifecycle: docs land at status: proposed when first authored, flip to status: active when the operator signals promotion, and stay in place — no directory move, no renumbering. The proposals-governance doc's statement that status: active is reserved for canon/decisions/ and canon/principles/ is narrowly true within the P/D track context; field-evidence shows the value also belongs on settled constraints and personas. A canon-edit clarifying this in ams://docs/proposals-governance is a worthwhile follow-up but out of scope for this PR.

column2: Journal TSVs are machine surfaces — no persona emoji
column3: This TSV is itself the first artifact to exercise the output-artifact validator's journal/**/*.tsv scope. Per klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide §Brand Guide §Machine Surfaces, persona emoji never appear on surfaces a parser will read. Journal TSVs are parsed (by humans, by the output-artifact validator, eventually by tooling). The 🦦 signature and the river vocabulary stay out. Functional status emoji (✅ ⚠️ 🔴 etc.) are informational rather than character and remain available — though this entry has no occasion to use them.

column2: Expected verdict matrix on this PR
column3: Legacy canon-code-sync-audit.yml (Managed Agents path, path-filtered to canon/** and code paths) — gates the PR; expected PASS once probes report. Runtime probe canon-code-sync-audit-runtime-probe.yml — fires on every PR; expected non-gating verdict comment with first parity datapoint against the legacy gate's verdict. Runtime probe oddkit-gauntlet-runtime-probe.yml — path-filtered to canon/**/*.md, writings/**/*.md, journal/**/*.md; fires because this PR touches all three (canon, journal). Expected verdict: oddkit_challenge mode=canon-tier-2 over the five changed canon files plus oddkit_audit URI integrity sweep, posted as non-gating verdict comment. Runtime probe output-artifact-validation-runtime-probe.yml — no path filter; fires on every PR. Audit scope is the persona's allow-list (currently journal/**/*.tsv against Dolcheo+ serialization-format). This PR includes exactly one in-scope file: this TSV. Expected verdict: PASS on Dolcheo+ format conformance.

column2: An anthropic_api_key_not_configured verdict from any probe means the dashboard secret did not deploy
column3: The ANTHROPIC_API_KEY was set as a Worker secret on production in a prior session. If any of the three runtime probes returns anthropic_api_key_not_configured in its verdict body, the dashboard secret did not deploy correctly. Operator-facing diagnostic path: dash.cloudflare.com → Workers → agent-messaging-service → Settings → Variables and Secrets. The error surfaces as a verdict-body diagnostic, not as a workflow-level failure, because the runtime probes are non-gating by design.

Post-merge: the four PR #86 canon docs are status: active and the substrate has produced its first real probe-parity data. The unrotated PAT and Anthropic API key in project instructions remain unrotated (standing security note; flagged once per session per project rules). Out of scope for this session and inherited by future sessions: wiring AMS gates as callers in klappy.dev (separate work); promoting any upstream klappy.dev canon (8 of 9 still at proposed; separate work); backfilling old AMS journal TSVs to canonical Dolcheo+ format (separate work). Next session reads: the verdict comments from this PR's three runtime probes (parity data), this journal entry, and the four newly-active canon docs as the binding contract for the gates going forward.
